{
  "name": "brain",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "brain": "cli/brain.ts"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "bun --watch --env-file=.env app/server.ts",
    "start": "bun run app/server.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "test": "bun test tests/unit/",
    "test:smoke": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/",
    "eval": "evalite run evals/",
    "eval:analytics": "evalite run evals/analytics.eval.ts",
    "eval:chat": "evalite run evals/chat-agent.eval.ts",
    "eval:watch": "evalite watch evals/",
    "smoke:phase1": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/phase1.test.ts",
    "smoke:logging": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/logging.test.ts",
    "smoke:onboarding": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/onboarding.test.ts",
    "smoke:readme-import": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/readme-import.test.ts",
    "smoke:extraction-quality": "bun test --env-file=.env tests/smoke/extraction-quality.test.ts",
    "migrate": "bun run --env-file=.env schema/migrate.ts",
    "build:cli": "bun build cli/brain.ts --compile --bytecode --minify --outfile brain"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@ai-sdk/devtools": "^0.0.15",
    "@ai-sdk/react": "^3.0.113",
    "@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk": "^0.2.71",
    "@better-auth/oauth-provider": "^1.5.3",
    "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk": "^1.27.1",
    "@openrouter/ai-sdk-provider": "^2.2.3",
    "@tanstack/react-router": "^1.131.24",
    "ai": "^6.0.101",
    "better-auth": "^1.5.3",
    "mdast-util-from-markdown": "^2.0.3",
    "mdast-util-gfm": "^3.1.0",
    "mdast-util-to-string": "^4.0.0",
    "micromark-extension-gfm": "^3.0.0",
    "pino": "^10.3.1",
    "react": "^19.1.1",
    "react-dom": "^19.1.1",
    "react-markdown": "^10.1.0",
    "reagraph": "^4.30.8",
    "reaviz": "^16.1.2",
    "surrealdb": "2.0.0-alpha.18",
    "zod": "^4.3.6",
    "zustand": "^5.0.11"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/bun": "^1.3.10",
    "@types/mdast": "^4.0.4",
    "@types/node": "^24.5.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.1.4",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.1.4",
    "autoevals": "^0.0.132",
    "bun-types": "^1.3.5",
    "dotenv": "^17.3.1",
    "evalite": "^0.19.0",
    "typescript": "^5.9.2",
    "vitest": "^4.0.18"
  }
}

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "types": ["node"],
    "typeRoots": ["./node_modules/@types", "./node_modules/bun-types"]
  },
  "include": ["app/**/*.ts", "app/**/*.tsx"]
}

file.content:
services:
  surrealdb:
    image: surrealdb/surrealdb:v3.0.0
    container_name: brain-surrealdb
    user: root
    command:
      - start
      - --user
      - root
      - --pass
      - root
      - --allow-guests
      - rocksdb:/data/brain.db
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    volumes:
      - surrealdb-data:/data
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: [CMD, /surreal, isready]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10

  surrealdb-init:
    image: surrealdb/surrealdb:v3.0.0
    container_name: brain-surrealdb-init
    depends_on:
      surrealdb:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - ./schema:/schema:ro
    entrypoint: ["/surreal"]
    command:
      - import
      - --endpoint
      - http://surrealdb:8000
      - --ns
      - brain
      - --db
      - app
      - -u
      - root
      - -p
      - root
      - /schema/surreal-schema.surql
    restart: "no"

volumes:
  surrealdb-data:

content.text:
Created `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with three parallel jobs on Ubicloud runners:

- **typecheck** — runs `tsc --noEmit`
- **unit-tests** — runs `bun test tests/unit/`
- **smoke-tests** — spins up SurrealDB v3.0 as a service container, runs smoke tests against it

All jobs use `ubicloud-standard-2` runners with Bun installed via `oven-sh/setup-bun@v2`. PR concurrency is set to cancel in-progress runs on new pushes.

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}

content.text:
The smoke tests need quite a few env vars. Which secrets do you want to configure? The server requires:

**API Keys (secrets):**
- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`

**Auth (secrets):**
- `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET`
- `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID`
- `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET`

**Model config (could be plain env vars):**
- `CHAT_AGENT_MODEL`, `EXTRACTION_MODEL`, `ANALYTICS_MODEL`
- `OPENROUTER_EMBEDDING_MODEL`, `EMBEDDING_DIMENSION`
- `EXTRACTION_STORE_THRESHOLD`, `EXTRACTION_DISPLAY_THRESHOLD`

**Infra (handled by the service container already):**
- `SURREAL_URL`, `SURREAL_USERNAME`, `SURREAL_PASSWORD`, `SURREAL_NAMESPACE`, `SURREAL_DATABASE`
- `PORT`, `BETTER_AUTH_URL`

Which of these do you want wired up as GitHub secrets vs hardcoded in the workflow? And should the smoke tests actually boot the full app server, or are they only hitting SurrealDB directly?

1→name: CI
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  pull_request:
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→
     9→concurrency:
    10→  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
    11→  cancel-in-progress: true
    12→
    13→jobs:
    14→  typecheck:
    15→    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-2
    16→    steps:
    17→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    18→      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
    19→      - run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
    20→      - run: bun run typecheck
    21→
    22→  unit-tests:
    23→    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-2
    24→    steps:
    25→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    26→      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
    27→      - run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
    28→      - run: bun test tests/unit/
    29→
    30→  smoke-tests:
    31→    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-8
    32→    services:
    33→      surrealdb:
    34→        image: surrealdb/surrealdb:v3.0.0
    35→        ports:
    36→          - 8000:8000
    37→        options: >-
    38→          --health-cmd "/surreal isready"
    39→          --health-interval 2s
    40→          --health-timeout 10s
    41→          --health-retries 10
    42→        env:
    43→          SURREAL_USER: root
    44→          SURREAL_PASS: root
    45→          SURREAL_ALLOW_GUESTS: "true"

file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  typecheck:
    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-2
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: bun run typecheck

  unit-tests:
    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-2
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: bun test tests/unit/

  smoke-tests:
    runs-on: ubicloud-standard-8
    services:
      surrealdb:
        image: surrealdb/surrealdb:v3.0.0
        ports:
          - 8000:8000
        options: >-
          --health-cmd "/surreal isready"
          --health-interval 2s
          --health-timeout 10s
          --health-retries 10
        env:
          SURREAL_USER: root
          SURREAL_PASS: root
          SURREAL_ALLOW_GUESTS: "true"
          SURREAL_PATH: memory
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: bun run test:smoke
        env:
          SURREAL_URL: http://localhost:8000
          SURREAL_USER: root
          SURREAL_PASS: root
          SURREAL_NAMESPACE: test
          SURREAL_DATABASE: test

data.command:
Review this conversation for items that were discussed but NOT logged via the Brain MCP tools.

IMPORTANT: If this was a trivial session (greetings, test messages, simple Q&A with no implementation work), immediately respond with:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"No substantive work to log"}
Do NOT explain why there is nothing to log.

Otherwise, check each category:

1. **Decisions**: Architecture or design choices where alternatives existed (e.g. choosing a pattern, library, data model, or trade-off). Do NOT log mechanical bugfixes, typo corrections, or changes with only one correct approach. Call create_provisional_decision for each genuine decision.
2. **Questions**: Unresolved questions raised but not logged. Call ask_question for each.
3. **Observations**: Contradictions, duplication, missing items, deprecated patterns, or anomalies noticed. Call log_observation for each.
4. **Task progress**: Tasks worked on whose status wasn't updated. Call update_task_status for each.
5. **Subtasks identified**: Work items or follow-up tasks discussed but not created. Call create_subtask for each (requires a parent task).
6. **Suggestions**: Optimizations, risks, opportunities, missing elements, or potential pivots noticed during work. Call create_suggestion for each with category (optimization|risk|opportunity|conflict|missing|pivot), rationale, and confidence (0-1).

After logging everything, respond with valid JSON in this exact shape:
{"decision":"approve","summary":"<one-line session summary>","decisions_made":["<decision-id>"],"questions_asked":["<question-id>"],"tasks_progressed":[{"task_id":"<task-id>","from_status":"<from>","to_status":"<to>"}],"files_changed":[{"path":"<path>","change_type":"created|modified|deleted"}],"observations_logged":["<observation-id>"],"subtasks_created":["<subtask-id>"],"suggestions_created":["<suggestion-id>"]}

If you cannot log required items (for example MCP unavailable), respond with:
{"decision":"block","reason":"Log these items first: ..."}
